$comment: Every background task in the backend, and how it dies (ADR-CORE-037, ADR-APP-032).
Spawning work creates an entry point: something starts running with nobody above it to report to.
A task that ends quietly — a loop that exits on the first Err, a worker that returns on a closed
channel without a word — is a silent death, and the user's first hint is a feature that stopped
working with nothing in the log.
The crash gate (run by `npm run lint`) counts the spawns in every Rust source file and rejects any
it does not find here. Adding a task therefore forces one question to be answered in writing: HOW
DOES THIS ONE DIE? Answer it in `why`.
This file is PROJECT-OWNED: never pinned, never delivered by `governance:update`.

src-tauri/src/lib.rs.why: (1) The log bridge: forwards tracing records to the UI log view. On RecvError::Lagged it warns and KEEPS BRIDGING (the receiver is still valid); on RecvError::Closed the sender is gone (logging is shutting down) and it logs that and ends. It never exits on the first error. (2) load_model_at_startup: a spawn_blocking that loads the user's model at startup; a missing model is a normal state (logged at info, the app runs on and offers a download), and a load failure is logged loudly (error) — the task then returns, never silently.

src-tauri/src/commands/mod.rs.why: Three spawn_blocking wrappers around slow model IO (download_model, import_model, set_model→load_model), each AWAITED by its own command. A JoinError (the blocking task panicked) is mapped to an AppError and returned to the caller — surfaced in the UI and logged at the IPC chokepoint; the inner ModelError is surfaced the same way. None of them can end silently: the command's caller always learns the outcome.

src-tauri/src/pushtotalk/win32/session.r.why: (1) start_level_pump: polls the recording input level ~20×/s and pushes it into the overlay; its one exit is recording_level() returning None (the recording ended), after which it settles the meter to rest and stops. (2) the worker-restart thread: reloads the model into a fresh worker after a transcription failure (ADR-PROJ-005); it logs the outcome (restarted, or could-not-restart) and ends. Neither loops forever nor dies without a word.
